evidence_slots.impact: If jtag_unlock can be asserted by an insufficiently authenticated debug/JTAG path, all register locks can be cleared, enabling later changes to access policy and protected peripheral registers.

evidence_slots.impact: A requester already permitted to access ACCT can rewrite policy to loosen access controls; reset defaults are permissive; external we_flag can bypass stored policy by forcing allow bits.

evidence_slots.observed_behavior: pkt_wrapper gates AXI activity by en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i. On writes, address 0 sets fuse_req_o from wdata[0] and address 1 sets fuse_addr_o from wdata, with no reglk_ctrl_i check at the write site. Read path returns pkey_loc halves unless reglk_ctrl_i[4]/[5] are set and returns fuse_rdata_i at case address 4 unless reglk_ctrl_i[6] is set. It also initializes rdata to fuse_rdata_i whenever en before case selection. pkt instance is driven with req_i(1'b1) and fuse_indx_i(fuse_addr_o). Top-level connects fuse_mem outputs and JTAG hashes to the same fuse memory source.

evidence_slots.expected_guard: software-triggered or NoC-visible reset controls for security-relevant peripherals should require privilege/access-control/lifecycle/debug authorization and should not clear locks/policies except under intended reset authority
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level declares rst_1..rst_13 and has a reset NoC interface. Wrappers consume rst_5/rst_6/rst_8/rst_9. pkt_wrapper reset clears fuse_req_o/fuse_addr_o; acct_wrapper reset/rst_6 sets acct_mem to 32'hffffffff (permissive policy); dma_wrapper reset/rst_8 clears DMA config/status registers; reglk_wrapper reset/rst_9 clears reglk_mem lock policy to zero. Evidence of reset-control write/decode authorization is not provided.
evidence_slots.path: NoC reset port -> rst_axi_req/rst logic (not fully shown) -> rst_1..rst_13 -> wrapper reset inputs rst_5/rst_6/rst_8/rst_9 -> protected/security state cleared or made permissive
evidence_slots.state_condition: rst_ni global reset and per-wrapper rst_* both affect wrapper state. rst_6 causes all access policy words to all ones; rst_9 clears lock words to zero; rst_8 clears DMA registers; rst_5 clears fuse request/address.
evidence_slots.impact: If peripheral resets are software-triggerable without strong authorization, an requester can clear locks/policies or force permissive defaults, affecting later access control and state integrity.

rtl_evidence.detail: declares rst_1 through rst_13 reset signals.
reset condition ~(rst_ni && ~rst_5) clears fuse_req_o and fuse_addr_o.
reset condition ~(rst_ni && ~rst_6) sets every acct_mem entry to 32'hffffffff.
reset condition ~(rst_ni && ~rst_8) clears DMA configuration registers.
reset condition including rst_9 clears all reglk_mem entries to zero.
reset AXI request tie-off excerpt confirms a reset NoC/AXI peripheral path exists, though decode logic is not shown.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Reset-control address decode and authorization logic around rst_* generation was not provided.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Software/NoC-visible peripheral reset controls appear security-sensitive because wrapper reset inputs rst_5/rst_6/rst_8/rst_9 clear fuse request/address, access policy, DMA config, and register locks; reset generation authorization is not evidenced, and rst_9 can clear lock policy.
inspection_records.notes: Candidate violation is driven by demonstrated security impact of rst_* plus absent guard evidence at reset generation.

evidence_slots.subject: NoC/MMIO requester reading security peripherals
evidence_slots.object: NoC response data for AES0/AES1/AES2/RSA/RNG/SHA256/HMAC and wrapper read mux data such as ariane_*_buf_noc3_data_o
evidence_slots.expected_guard: readback from crypto/security peripherals should expose only intended status/results and be mediated by access-control/lock; internal keys/intermediate state should not be directly routed to NoC/debug read paths
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: Top-level instantiates crypto/security wrappers and NoC AXI-lite bridges. RSA and HMAC excerpts show wrapper acct_ctrl_i from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][index], reglk_ctrl_i slices, debug_mode_i, and AXI response r.data routed to NoC3 data through the bridge. Search results show analogous acc_ctrl_c connections for AES/RNG/SHA/HMAC/RSA. The actual wrapper read mux/data classification is not in provided files.
evidence_slots.path: NoC2 crypto/security request ports -> noc_axilite_bridge -> wrapper AXI req -> wrapper read response data -> noc_axilite_bridge -> ariane_*_buf_noc3_data_o/valid_o
evidence_slots.state_condition: Wrapper access is intended to depend on acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] and reglk_ctrl_i; debug_mode_i is passed to several crypto wrappers but its effect is unknown; response valid/data gating is in bridge/wrappers not fully shown.
evidence_slots.impact: Potential exposure of keys/intermediate/stale crypto state cannot be ruled out, but the available evidence only proves the existence of access-controlled wrapper connections, not unsafe readback.

rtl_evidence.detail: RSA wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i slice, acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][13]), debug_mode_i, and AXI req/resp.
RSA NoC AXI-lite bridge routes rsa_axi_resp.r.data/r_valid to ariane_rsa_buf_noc3_data_o/valid_o.
HMAC wrapper receives reglk_ctrl_i slice, acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][4]), debug_mode_i, and AXI req/resp.
HMAC bridge routes hmac_axi_resp.r.data/r_valid to NoC response signals.
acc_ctrl_c is connected to wrapper indices for multiple crypto/security wrappers; debug_mode_i appears on selected wrappers.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: AES/RSA/RNG/SHA/HMAC wrapper read muxes, internal key/state classifications, and debug_mode_i effects are not included.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: Crypto/security NoC read paths are routed through wrappers that receive acc_ctrl_c and reglk_ctrl slices, but wrapper internals and read muxes for AES/RSA/RNG/SHA/HMAC were not provided; no direct key/intermediate-state exposure can be confirmed.
inspection_records.notes: Record remains inconclusive rather than candidate violation because guard connections exist at wrapper boundaries and unsafe readback is not demonstrated.

28:     input  ariane_axi::req_t       axi_req_i;

41: logic [AXI_ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] address;
42: logic                      en, en_acct;
43: logic                      we;
44: logic [63:0] wdata;
45: logic [63:0] rdata;
46: 
47: 
48: ///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
49:     // -----------------------------
50:     // AXI Interface Logic
51:     // -----------------------------
52:     axi_lite_interface #(
53:         .AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ( AXI_ADDR_WIDTH ),
54:         .AXI_DATA_WIDTH ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH ),
55:         .AXI_ID_WIDTH   ( AXI_ID_WIDTH    )
56:     ) axi_lite_interface_i (
57:         .clk_i      ( clk_i      ),
58:         .rst_ni     ( rst_ni     ),

60:         .axi_resp_o ( axi_resp_o ),
61:         .address_o  ( address    ),
62:         .en_o       ( en_acct    ),

74:     begin

76:             begin
77:                 fuse_req_o <= 0;
78:                 fuse_addr_o <= 0;
79:             end
80:         else if(en && we)
81:             case(address[7:3])
82:                 0:
83:                     fuse_req_o  <= wdata[0];
84:                 1:
85:                     fuse_addr_o <= wdata;
86:                 default:
87:                     ;
88:             endcase
89:     end // always @ (posedge wb_clk_i)
90: 
91: //// Read side
92: always @(*)
93:     begin
94:       rdata = 64'b0; 
95:       if (en) begin
96:         rdata = fuse_rdata_i; 
97:         case(address[7:3])
98:             0:
99:                 rdata = {31'b0, fuse_req_o};
100:             1:
101:                 rdata = fuse_addr_o;
102:             2:
103:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[4] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[63:32];
104:             3:
105:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[5] ? 'b0 : pkey_loc[31:0];
106:             4:
107:                 rdata = reglk_ctrl_i[6] ? 'b0 : fuse_rdata_i;
108:             default:
109:                 if (fuse_addr_o <= 110)
110:                     rdata = 32'b0;
111:         endcase
112:       end // if
113:     end // always @ (*)
114: 
115: pkt # (
116:         .FUSE_MEM_SIZE(FUSE_MEM_SIZE)
117:     ) i_pkt(
118:             .clk_i(clk_i),
119:             .rst_ni(rst_ni && ~rst_5),
120:             .req_i(1'b1),
121:             .fuse_indx_i(fuse_addr_o),
122:             .pkey_loc_o(pkey_loc)
123:         );
